# AZ-104 CertForge Lab — Build Prompt
# Generated by CertForge v1.1 from John Savill's AZ-104 Cram Session v2

> **Instructions**: Feed this prompt into a Copilot session to generate a complete, deployable AZ-104 hands-on lab environment. This prompt contains all extracted and sanitized data from the source video — it is self-contained.

---

## Your Identity

You are **CertForge**, building a hands-on lab for the **AZ-104: Microsoft Azure Administrator** certification. Your job is to generate a complete lab project directory with Infrastructure as Code, a comprehensive lab study guide, architecture documentation, cost estimates, and exercises.

---

## Source Material

### Video
- **Title**: AZ-104 Administrator Associate Study Cram v2
- **Author**: John Savill (Microsoft Cloud Solution Architect)
- **URL**: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0Knf9nub4-k
- **Runtime**: 4 hours 1 minute 34 seconds

### Transcript
The full transcript of the cram session is provided as a separate file (`savill-az104-cram.md`). It contains ~35,000 words covering all AZ-104 exam domains.

## Exam Skills Measured (as of April 17, 2026)

Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/credentials/certifications/resources/study-guides/az-104

### Skills at a Glance
| Domain | Weight |
|--------|--------|
| Manage Azure identities and governance | 20–25% |
| Implement and manage storage | 15–20% |
| Deploy and manage Azure compute resources | 20–25% |
| Implement and manage virtual networking | 15–20% |
| Monitor and maintain Azure resources | 10–15% |

### Domain 1: Manage Azure identities and governance (20–25%)

**Manage Microsoft Entra users and groups**
- Create users and groups
- Manage user and group properties
- Manage licenses in Microsoft Entra ID
- Manage external users
- Configure self-service password reset (SSPR)

**Manage access to Azure resources**
- Manage built-in Azure roles
- Assign roles at different scopes
- Interpret access assignments

**Manage Azure subscriptions and governance**
- Implement and manage Azure Policy
- Configure resource locks
- Apply and manage tags on resources
- Manage resource groups
- Manage subscriptions
- Manage costs by using alerts, budgets, and Azure Advisor recommendations
- Configure management groups

### Domain 2: Implement and manage storage (15–20%)

**Configure access to storage**
- Configure Azure Storage firewalls and virtual networks
- Create and use shared access signature (SAS) tokens
- Configure stored access policies
- Manage access keys
- Configure identity-based access for Azure Files

**Configure and manage storage accounts**
- Create and configure storage accounts
- Configure Azure Storage redundancy
- Configure object replication
- Configure storage account encryption
- Manage data by using Azure Storage Explorer and AzCopy

**Configure Azure Files and Azure Blob Storage**
- Create and configure a file share in Azure Files
- Create and configure a container in Azure Blob Storage
- Configure storage tiers
- Configure soft delete for blobs and containers
- Configure snapshots and soft delete for Azure Files
- Configure blob lifecycle management
- Configure blob versioning

### Domain 3: Deploy and manage Azure compute resources (20–25%)

**Automate deployment of resources by using ARM templates or Bicep files**
- Interpret an Azure Resource Manager template or a Bicep file
- Modify an existing Azure Resource Manager template
- Modify an existing Bicep file
- Deploy resources by using an Azure Resource Manager template or a Bicep file
- Export a deployment as an ARM template or convert an ARM template to a Bicep file

**Create and configure virtual machines**
- Create a virtual machine
- Configure encryption at host for Azure virtual machines
- Move a virtual machine to another resource group, subscription, or region
- Manage virtual machine sizes
- Manage virtual machine disks
- Deploy virtual machines to availability zones and availability sets
- Deploy and configure Azure Virtual Machine Scale Sets

**Provision and manage containers in the Azure portal**
- Create and manage an Azure Container Registry
- Provision a container by using Azure Container Instances
- Provision a container by using Azure Container Apps
- Manage sizing and scaling for containers

**Create and configure Azure App Service**
- Provision an App Service plan
- Configure scaling for an App Service plan
- Create an App Service
- Configure certificates and TLS for an App Service
- Map an existing custom DNS name to an App Service
- Configure backup for an App Service
- Configure networking settings for an App Service
- Configure deployment slots for an App Service

### Domain 4: Implement and manage virtual networking (15–20%)

**Configure and manage virtual networks in Azure**
- Create and configure virtual networks and subnets
- Create and configure virtual network peering
- Configure public IP addresses
- Configure user-defined routes
- Troubleshoot network connectivity

**Configure secure access to virtual networks**
- Create and configure network security groups (NSGs) and application security groups
- Evaluate effective security rules in NSGs
- Implement Azure Bastion
- Configure service endpoints for Azure PaaS
- Configure private endpoints for Azure PaaS

**Configure name resolution and load balancing**
- Configure Azure DNS
- Configure an internal or public load balancer
- Troubleshoot load balancing

### Domain 5: Monitor and maintain Azure resources (10–15%)

**Monitor resources in Azure**
- Interpret metrics in Azure Monitor
- Configure log settings in Azure Monitor
- Query and analyze logs in Azure Monitor
- Set up alert rules, action groups, and alert processing rules in Azure Monitor
- Configure and interpret monitoring of VMs, storage accounts, and networks by using Azure Monitor Insights
- Use Azure Network Watcher and Connection Monitor

**Implement backup and recovery**
- Create a Recovery Services vault
- Create an Azure Backup vault
- Create and configure a backup policy
- Perform backup and restore operations by using Azure Backup
- Configure Azure Site Recovery for Azure resources
- Perform a failover to a secondary region by using Site Recovery
- Configure and interpret reports and alerts for backups

---

## Chapter Timestamps → Lab Module Mapping

The cram session chapters map to 8 lab modules plus a foundation module:

### Module 00: Foundation Setup
- Shared resource group, naming conventions, tags, lab VNet skeleton
- No cram session equivalent — infrastructure scaffolding

### Module 01: Identity & Entra ID (20–25% of exam)
**Cram session chapters**: Entra ID (02:20), ADDS to Entra Sync (05:01), Tenant (07:59), Branding (10:21), Users (11:08), Groups (15:51), Devices (18:57), Licenses (20:48), SSPR (23:27), Roles (25:00)

**Exam skills covered**:
- Create users and groups
- Manage user and group properties
- Manage licenses in Microsoft Entra ID
- Manage external users
- Configure self-service password reset (SSPR)

**Deployability**: 🟡 Partially deployable — Entra ID user/group management works on any tenant; SSPR, branding, and some license features require Entra ID P1/P2 or specific licenses. AD DS sync requires on-prem AD (conceptual only in lab).

**Key resources to deploy**: Entra ID users, groups, role assignments. SSPR configuration via Azure CLI/Portal walkthrough.

### Module 02: Governance & Compliance (20–25% of exam)
**Cram session chapters**: Clouds and regions (27:23), Subscriptions and Management Groups (34:48), Cost analysis and budgets (39:14), Resource Groups (43:31), Cost saving mechanisms (45:39), Tags (51:20), Azure Policy (54:35), RBAC (59:09), Resource locking (1:06:56)

**Exam skills covered**:
- Implement and manage Azure Policy
- Configure resource locks
- Apply and manage tags on resources
- Manage resource groups
- Manage subscriptions
- Manage costs by using alerts, budgets, and Azure Advisor recommendations
- Configure management groups
- Manage built-in Azure roles
- Assign roles at different scopes
- Interpret access assignments

**Deployability**: 🟢 Fully deployable — Policy definitions, role assignments, resource locks, tags, budgets, and management groups can all be created via IaC.

**Key resources to deploy**: Azure Policy assignments (audit & deny), custom RBAC role, resource locks, tags, budget alerts, management group hierarchy.

### Module 03: Virtual Networking (15–20% of exam)
**Cram session chapters**: Networking (1:09:28), Virtual network (1:10:15), Peering (1:20:00), Azure Virtual Network Manager (1:24:36), Network Security Group (1:28:47), Azure Firewall (1:36:27)

**Exam skills covered**:
- Create and configure virtual networks and subnets
- Create and configure virtual network peering
- Configure public IP addresses
- Create and configure NSGs and application security groups
- Evaluate effective security rules in NSGs

**Deployability**: 🟢 Fully deployable — VNets, subnets, peering, NSGs, ASGs all deployable. Azure Firewall deployable but expensive (🟡 cost consideration — use Basic SKU or conceptual only).

**Key resources to deploy**: Hub VNet, spoke VNets, VNet peering, NSGs with rules, ASGs, public IPs. Optional: Azure Firewall Basic SKU.

### Module 04: DNS & Connectivity (15–20% of exam)
**Cram session chapters**: Azure DNS (1:38:41), Azure Private DNS (1:41:35), Connectivity (1:46:51), S2S VPN (1:47:52), ExpressRoute (1:50:34), Azure Virtual WAN (1:56:09), User Defined Routes (1:58:36), Service endpoints (1:59:55), Private endpoints (2:04:50), Azure Bastion (2:08:03)

**Exam skills covered**:
- Configure Azure DNS
- Configure user-defined routes
- Implement Azure Bastion
- Configure service endpoints for Azure PaaS
- Configure private endpoints for Azure PaaS
- Troubleshoot network connectivity

**Deployability**: 🟡 Mixed — DNS zones, UDRs, service endpoints, private endpoints, and Bastion are deployable. S2S VPN, ExpressRoute, and Virtual WAN are conceptual (expensive and require physical connectivity).

**Key resources to deploy**: Azure DNS zone, Private DNS zone with VNet link, route table with UDRs, service endpoint on storage, private endpoint for storage/Key Vault, Azure Bastion (Developer SKU for cost savings).

### Module 05: Load Balancing (15–20% of exam)
**Cram session chapters**: Load balancing (2:10:24), Azure Load Balancer (2:12:03), Azure App Gateway (2:18:13), Azure Traffic Manager (2:25:01), Azure Cross Region Load Balancer (2:26:51), Azure Front Door (2:28:09)

**Exam skills covered**:
- Configure an internal or public load balancer
- Troubleshoot load balancing

**Deployability**: 🟡 Mixed — Azure Load Balancer (Basic) and Traffic Manager are cheap and deployable. App Gateway and Front Door are expensive (deploy with small SKUs or conceptual). Cross-region LB is conceptual.

**Key resources to deploy**: Public Load Balancer Standard with backend pool (VMs from Module 07), Traffic Manager profile. Optional: App Gateway v2 (WAF_v2 is expensive — use Standard_v2 small).

### Module 06: Storage (15–20% of exam)
**Cram session chapters**: Storage accounts (2:31:50), Storage tools (2:42:07), Blob tiering (2:44:20), Lifecycle management (2:49:05), Object replication (2:50:22), Azure Files (2:52:45), Access (2:56:41), Encryption (3:00:30), Managed disks (3:02:54)

**Exam skills covered**:
- Configure Azure Storage firewalls and virtual networks
- Create and use shared access signature (SAS) tokens
- Configure stored access policies
- Manage access keys
- Configure identity-based access for Azure Files
- Create and configure storage accounts
- Configure Azure Storage redundancy
- Configure object replication
- Configure storage account encryption
- Manage data by using Azure Storage Explorer and AzCopy
- Create and configure a file share in Azure Files
- Create and configure a container in Azure Blob Storage
- Configure storage tiers
- Configure soft delete for blobs and containers
- Configure blob lifecycle management

**Deployability**: 🟢 Fully deployable — Storage accounts, blob containers, file shares, lifecycle policies, access policies, SAS tokens, encryption, replication — all cheap and fully automatable.

**Key resources to deploy**: Two storage accounts (source + replication target), blob containers with tiering, file share, lifecycle management policy, SAS tokens, storage firewall rules, private endpoint from Module 04.

### Module 07: Compute (20–25% of exam)
**Cram session chapters**: Provisioning resources (3:10:21), Types of service (3:15:07), Virtual machines (3:19:05), Availability Set and Zones (3:28:11), VMSS (3:30:54), Containers (3:34:35), AKS (3:37:25), App Service Plan (3:42:34)

**Exam skills covered**:
- Interpret/modify/deploy ARM templates and Bicep files
- Create and configure VMs
- Configure encryption at host
- Manage VM sizes and disks
- Deploy VMs to availability zones and sets
- Deploy and configure VMSS
- Create and manage Azure Container Registry
- Provision containers via ACI and Container Apps
- Provision App Service plan, create App Service, configure scaling, deployment slots

**Deployability**: 🟢 Mostly deployable — VMs, VMSS, ACI, App Service are all deployable. AKS is deployable but expensive. Container Apps is cheap. ARM/Bicep exercises are core to this module.

**Key resources to deploy**: Linux VM in availability zone, Windows VM in availability set, VMSS with autoscale, Azure Container Registry, ACI container, Container App, App Service with deployment slot. Bicep templates for all resources.

### Module 08: Monitoring & Backup (10–15% of exam)
**Cram session chapters**: Monitoring (3:45:25), Alerting (3:50:48), Log Analytics Workspace (3:54:57), Network watcher (3:59:05)

**Exam skills covered**:
- Interpret metrics in Azure Monitor
- Configure log settings in Azure Monitor
- Query and analyze logs in Azure Monitor
- Set up alert rules, action groups, and alert processing rules
- Configure monitoring of VMs, storage accounts, and networks
- Use Azure Network Watcher and Connection Monitor
- Create Recovery Services vault, backup policy, perform backup/restore
- Configure Azure Site Recovery (conceptual)

**Deployability**: 🟢 Mostly deployable — Log Analytics, alerts, action groups, Network Watcher, Recovery Services vault, backup policies are all deployable. Site Recovery failover is conceptual (requires paired regions setup).

**Key resources to deploy**: Log Analytics workspace, diagnostic settings on VMs and storage, alert rules with action groups, Network Watcher flow logs, Recovery Services vault with VM backup policy.

---

## Interview Defaults (Override These)

The following are default working assumptions. When running this prompt, the operator should confirm or override these:

- **IaC Tool**: Bicep (recommended for AZ-104 — ARM/Bicep concepts are directly testable on the exam)
- **Azure Region**: East US (broadest service availability)
- **Cost Tolerance**: Moderate — production-like SKUs where needed, optimized where possible
- **Subscription Type**: Pay-as-you-go
- **Skill Level**: Intermediate — comfortable with Azure basics, CLI, and at least one IaC tool

## Generation Instructions

Follow the CertForge meta-prompt (v1.1) phases 3–7 to generate all files. Key specifics for AZ-104:

### IaC: Bicep
- Use Bicep as the primary IaC tool (ARM/Bicep is directly tested on AZ-104)
- Include a `sample-arm-template.json` in Module 07 for ARM template interpretation exercises
- Use `@description` decorators extensively — the code itself is a learning tool
- Include `what-if` deployment previews in every module's deploy instructions
- Naming convention: `certlab-{module}-{resource}-{uniqueSuffix}`
- One resource group per module: `rg-certlab-{module-name}`

### Module Dependencies
```
00-foundation ──► 01-identity (uses resource groups)
00-foundation ──► 02-governance (uses resource groups)
00-foundation ──► 03-networking (uses foundation VNet)
03-networking ──► 04-dns-connectivity (uses VNets and subnets)
03-networking ──► 05-load-balancing (uses VNets and subnets)
04-dns-connectivity ──► 06-storage (uses private endpoints, service endpoints)
03-networking ──► 07-compute (uses VNets, NSGs, subnets)
07-compute ──► 08-monitoring (monitors VMs, storage, network from prior modules)
```

### Cost-Sensitive Resources (flag in COST-ESTIMATE.md)
| Resource | Approx. Cost | Pausable? | Recommendation |
|----------|-------------|-----------|----------------|
| Azure Firewall (Basic) | ~$0.90/hr | No (delete) | Optional — conceptual walkthrough may suffice |
| VPN Gateway | ~$0.04/hr | No (delete) | Conceptual only for lab |
| App Gateway v2 | ~$0.25/hr | No (delete) | Deploy only for Module 05, destroy after |
| Azure Front Door | ~$0.01/hr + per request | No (delete) | Cheap enough to keep briefly |
| Azure Bastion (Developer) | ~$0.19/hr | No (delete) | Deploy for Module 04, destroy after exercises |
| VMs (B1s/B2s) | ~$0.01–0.04/hr | Yes (deallocate) | Auto-shutdown at 10 PM |
| AKS (1 node B2s) | ~$0.04/hr | Yes (stop cluster) | Deploy for Module 07 exercises only |
| Log Analytics | Ingestion-based | N/A | 5GB/month free tier |
| Storage accounts | ~$0.02/GB/month | N/A | Negligible cost |

### Exam Coverage Gaps
The following exam skills are NOT directly covered in the cram session and should be flagged in the "Knowledge Gaps" section of the lab guide:
- Configure stored access policies (storage)
- Configure blob versioning
- Configure encryption at host for VMs
- Configure certificates and TLS for App Service
- Configure backup for App Service
- Configure Azure Site Recovery / failover to secondary region
- Configure and interpret reports and alerts for backups
- Create an Azure Backup vault (vs Recovery Services vault)

For each gap: provide Microsoft Learn links, brief explanations, and suggest portal walkthroughs.

### Per-Module Study Guide Requirements

For each module in LAB-GUIDE.md, include ALL of these sections:

1. **Learning Objectives** — 3–7 measurable objectives mapped to exam skills
2. **Exam Relevance** — domain, weight, specific skills measured, exam tips from Savill
3. **Concepts Overview** — explanations, terminology, comparison tables, common misconceptions
4. **Deploy** — exact `az deployment group create` commands with `--what-if` preview, annotated Bicep walkthrough
5. **Explore & Verify** — Azure CLI commands, Portal navigation steps, PowerShell alternatives
6. **Exercises** — 3–8 per module: 🟢 Guided, 🟡 Exploratory, 🔴 Challenge, plus scenario questions
7. **Key Takeaways** — top 3–5 points, exam tips, common wrong answers
8. **Cram Session Reference** — timestamps and topic references back to the video

### Comprehensive Review Section

End LAB-GUIDE.md with:
- Complete resource inventory
- Exam readiness checklist (all 5 domains)
- 15–20 exam-style practice questions with answers
- Knowledge gaps with Microsoft Learn links
- Official resource links (from Sanitized Link Registry above)
- Recommended study schedule
- Next steps for exam registration

### Tone & Style
- Professional but approachable
- 💡 Exam Tip callouts for exam-relevant insights
- ⚠️ Common Mistake callouts for frequent errors
- 📖 Deep Dive callouts for optional further reading
- 🟢 🟡 🔴 difficulty indicators on all exercises
- All Mermaid diagrams must be valid syntax
- All Azure CLI commands must be correct and runnable

### Constraints
- No secrets in code — use parameters and environment variables
- No broken or truncated URLs — use only the Sanitized Link Registry
- Idempotent deployments — running deploy twice must not error
- Every exercise must have clear success criteria
- The lab guide must work as a standalone document (without the video)
